title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple reflects on her companion, Miss Knight, a fifty-six-year-old woman suffering from bronchitis. Although Miss Knight is kind, Miss Marple finds her patronizing, particularly regarding her habit of calling Miss Marple "dear." Despite Dr. Haydock’s previous warning that Miss Knight should sleep in a shared room due to her illness, Miss Marple finds the current living arrangement tolerable.

When Miss Knight prepares to go for a walk, Miss Marple provides her with an extensive list of errands designed to keep her occupied for a long duration. The list includes checking curtains at a London store, purchasing blue yarn from Mrs. Willett, buying blackcurrant lozenges at a drugstore, exchanging a book at the library, and inquiring about a hand-operated whisk mixer at Harritz’s. Miss Marple intentionally includes the request for the mixer, knowing such an item does not exist in the local market.

Once Miss Knight has departed, Miss Marple waits until she is sufficiently far away before changing into outdoor clothes to take her own walk. During her stroll, Miss Marple observes Mr. Lecock pruning roses and compares Miss Knight’s simple-minded nature to a woman named Emily Waters. Miss Marple follows a path through the vicarage garden, passing an iron gate and a bridge over a stream to reach a nearby residential development.
